title: Getting error  unable to mkdir /var/run/s6: Permission denied if i run --userns=keep-id
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

I am not sure, it was working fine on my previous server, but now i setup a new server and i am getting this weird permission 
issue..

I am using --userns=keep-id so that i can give my user access to delete folders thats are -v in the docker, without the need of using podman unshare or root access.

The command i am running

`podman run -dt --name=deluge --net=host --security-opt label=disable -e PUID=$(id -u) -e PGID=$(id -g) --userns=keep-id -e UMASK_SET=022 -v ~/.config/deluge:/config -v ~/downloads/deluge:/downloads docker.io/linuxserver/deluge
`
However, i get the follow, it works fine if i remove --userns=keep-id but then the user is unable to delete the files or folders.


**Describe the results you expected:**
Should be able to run the docker without getting permission denied issue

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

```

```

comments.body:
This my understanding of the matter, please correct me if I'm wrong.

Quote from the __podman run__ [man page](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-run.1.md#--usernsmode)


"_keep-id: creates a user namespace where the current rootless user’s UID:GID are mapped to the same values in the container. This option is ignored for containers created by the root user._"

Something that is not mentioned is that the command-line option  `--userns=keep-id` will also run the container as the added user (i.e. `--user $(id -u):$(id -g)` is implied).


#### Demo 1: Showing that "_--userns=keep-id_" implies "_--user $(id -u):$(id -g)_"

```
$ id -u
1004
$ podman run --rm -ti --userns keep-id docker.io/library/fedora id -u
1004
$ id -g
1004
$ podman run --rm -ti --userns keep-id docker.io/library/fedora id -g
1004
$
```


~Running `chown` to change user ownership of a file will require root permissions (UID=0, GID=0), so generally it does not make sense to use `chown` together with `--userns=keep-id`.~

__Update 2021-10-24. I would like to rephrase the previous sentence__ : _Running `chown` to change user ownership of a file will require root permissions (UID=0, GID=0), so generally it does not make sense to use `chown` together with `--userns=keep-id` without also using `--user 0:0`._ (See https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12068#issuecomment-950268443)


(Using `chown` to change group ownership to any supplementary group is allowed even without root permission, but I leave that out of this discussion)

@sonuyos In the output you provide
```
fatal: unable to chown /var/run/s6/etc/cont-init.d/99-custom-scripts: Operation not permitted
```

it looks like `chown` is failing.

Considering the extra environment variables `-e PUID=$(id -u) -e PGID=$(id -g)` and that they should influence the ownership of the files and directories when running the container, I would guess that the authors of the container image expect that the container is meant to be run with rootful docker/podman and and run as root (UID=0, GID=0) inside the container.

Instead of using **--userns=keep-id**, you could try using **--uidmap**  and **--gidmap** instead. You could then run the container with root inside the container but at the same time have the UID `$PUID` inside the container mapped to the UID `$PUID` on the host.

Does it work if you instead of


```
podman run -dt \
             --name=deluge \
             --net=host \
             --security-opt \
             label=disable \
             -e PUID=$(id -u) \
             -e PGID=$(id -g) \
             --userns=keep-id \
             -e UMASK_SET=022 \
             -v ~/.config/deluge:/config \
             -v ~/downloads/deluge:/downloads \
             docker.io/linuxserver/deluge
```
run these commands
```
ctr_uid=$(id -u)
ctr_gid=$(id -g)
subuidSize=65536
subgidSize=65536
podman run -dt \
             --name=deluge \
             --net=host \
             --security-opt \
             label=disable \
             -e PUID=$(id -u) \
             -e PGID=$(id -g) \
             -e UMASK_SET=022 \
             --uidmap=0:1:$ctr_uid \
             --uidmap=$(($ctr_uid + 1)):$(($ctr_uid + 1)):$(($subuidSize - $ctr_uid - 1)) \
             --uidmap=${ctr_uid}:0:1 \
             --gidmap=0:1:$ctr_gid \
             --gidmap=$(($ctr_gid + 1)):$(($ctr_gid + 1)):$(($subgidSize - $ctr_gid - 1)) \
             --gidmap=${ctr_gid}:0:1 \
             -v ~/.config/deluge:/config \
             -v ~/downloads/deluge:/downloads \
             docker.io/linuxserver/deluge
```
?

The difference of the commands shown as a diff:

``` diff
--- /tmp/old  2021-10-23 20:36:09.549036145 +0200
+++ /tmp/new   2021-10-23 20:40:39.034037850 +0200
@@ -1,3 +1,7 @@
+ctr_uid=$(id -u)
+ctr_gid=$(id -g)
+subuidSize=65536
+subgidSize=65536
 podman run -dt \
              --name=deluge \
              --net=host \
@@ -5,8 +9,13 @@
              label=disable \
              -e PUID=$(id -u) \
              -e PGID=$(id -g) \
-             --userns=keep-id \
              -e UMASK_SET=022 \
+             --uidmap=0:1:$ctr_uid \
+             --uidmap=$(($ctr_uid + 1)):$(($ctr_uid + 1)):$(($subuidSize - $ctr_uid - 1)) \
+             --uidmap=${ctr_uid}:0:1 \
+             --gidmap=0:1:$ctr_gid \
+             --gidmap=$(($ctr_gid + 1)):$(($ctr_gid + 1)):$(($subgidSize - $ctr_gid - 1)) \
+             --gidmap=${ctr_gid}:0:1 \
              -v ~/.config/deluge:/config \
              -v ~/downloads/deluge:/downloads \
              docker.io/linuxserver/deluge
```


#### Demo 2: Using `--uidmap` and `--gidmap` to map the host user UID/GID to the same UID/GID inside the container.

The shell variable `ctr_uid` represents the UID inside the container that the host user UID should be mapped to.
`ctr_uid` could be set to an arbitrary UID. Choosing  `ctr_uid` to be the same as the host user UID is just a special case

```
ctr_uid=$(id -u)     # This is just a special case
```

Running these commands
```
mkdir dir1
ctr_uid=$(id -u)
ctr_gid=$(id -g)
subuidSize=65536
subgidSize=65536
podman run  \
             --uidmap=0:1:$ctr_uid \
             --uidmap=$(($ctr_uid + 1)):$(($ctr_uid + 1)):$(($subuidSize - $ctr_uid - 1)) \
             --uidmap=${ctr_uid}:0:1 \
             --gidmap=0:1:$ctr_gid \
             --gidmap=$(($ctr_gid + 1)):$(($ctr_gid + 1)):$(($subgidSize - $ctr_gid - 1)) \
             --gidmap=${ctr_gid}:0:1 \
             -v ./dir1:/ctrdir1:Z \
             docker.io/library/fedora bash -c \
            "id; touch /ctrdir1/testfile ; chown $ctr_uid:$ctr_gid /ctrdir1/testfile ; ls -ln /ctrdir1/testfile"
ls -ln dir1/testfile
```
produces this output
```
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
-rw-r--r--. 1 1004 1004 0 Oct 23 19:08 /ctrdir1/testfile
-rw-r--r--. 1 1004 1004 0 Oct 23 21:08 dir1/testfile
```

Note that the container was run rootless but with root inside the container. It managed to create a file with ownership mapped to the user on the host.

Reference:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-run.1.md#--uidmapcontainer_uidfrom_uidamount



#### Additional note
The  shell variables _ctr_uid_, _ctr_gid_, _subuidSize_,  _subgidSize_ used in the examples above do not have any meaning outside of these examples.  I added them to make the examples easier to read.

Hi @eriksjolund thanks for the massive input, i will give it a try, however i was able to solve it by using `--userns=host` instead of `--userns=keep-id` and it did work the last time i tried it, but i am so sleep deprived currently, that i am unable to test it further, i will keep you updated once i test it tomorrow evening.

I just realized, it's possible to combine `--userns=keep-id` with `--user=0:0`.

#### Demo 3. Combining  `--userns=keep-id` with `--user=0:0`.

```
mkdir dir1
ctr_uid=$(id -u)
ctr_gid=$(id -g)
podman run  \
             --userns=keep-id \
             --user 0:0 \
             -v ./dir1:/ctrdir1:Z \
             docker.io/library/fedora bash -c \
            "id; touch /ctrdir1/testfile ; chown $ctr_uid:$ctr_gid /ctrdir1/testfile ; ls -ln /ctrdir1/testfile"
ls -ln dir1/testfile
```
produces this output
```
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
-rw-r--r--. 1 1004 1004 0 Oct 23 19:08 /ctrdir1/testfile
-rw-r--r--. 1 1004 1004 0 Oct 23 21:08 dir1/testfile
```

#### New suggestion (much simpler)

Try adding `--user 0:0`

(no need to use __--uidmap__ and __--gidmap__).

> I just realized, it's possible to combine `--userns=keep-id` with `--user=0:0`.
> 
> #### Demo 3. Combining `--userns=keep-id` with `--user=0:0`.
> ```
> mkdir dir1
> ctr_uid=$(id -u)
> ctr_gid=$(id -g)
> podman run  \
>              --userns=keep-id \
>              --user 0:0 \
>              -v ./dir1:/ctrdir1:Z \
>              docker.io/library/fedora bash -c \
>             "id; touch /ctrdir1/testfile ; chown $ctr_uid:$ctr_gid /ctrdir1/testfile ; ls -ln /ctrdir1/testfile"
> ls -ln dir1/testfile
> ```
> 
> produces this output
> 
> ```
> uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
> -rw-r--r--. 1 1004 1004 0 Oct 23 19:08 /ctrdir1/testfile
> -rw-r--r--. 1 1004 1004 0 Oct 23 21:08 dir1/testfile
> ```
> 
> #### New suggestion (much simpler)
> Try adding `--user 0:0`
> 
> (no need to use **--uidmap** and **--gidmap**).

Hi Erik, Unfortunately, this does not work, i dont know why.

`podman run --name=headphones -dt --systemd=false --security-opt label=disable -e PUID=$(id -u) -e PGID=$(id -g) --userns=host --user=0:0 -e UMASK_SET=022 -p $PORT:8181 -v ~/.config/headphones:/config -v ~/downloads/headphones/:/downloads docker.io/linuxserver/headphones
`

`drwxr-xr-x 6 166536 166536 4.0K Oct 24 20:27 headphones
`

@eriksjolund sorry i am a fing idiot.

I ran wrong command, ran it with userns=host instead of keep-ids, seems to be working, thanks, i will monitor and update soon.
Thanks @eriksjolund @sonuyos Closing this one. Seems to be incorrect flag usage which is resolved. Please re-open if something is not resolved.
